author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew 1. 22, 23.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the Prophet, saying, Behold, a Virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a Son, and they shall call His name Emmanuel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I Hear many say, “While we are here, and enjoying the privilege of hearing, we are awed, but when we are gone out, we become altered men again, and the flame of zeal is quenched.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then may be done, that this may not come to pass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us observe whence it arises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then doth so great a change in us arise?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From the unbecoming employment of our time, and from the company of evil men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we ought not as soon as we retire from the Communion,to plunge into business unsuited to the Communion, but as soon as ever we get home, to take our Bible into our hands, and call our wife and children to join us in putting together what we have heard, and then, not before, engage in the business of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if after the bath you would not choose to hurry into the market place, lest by the business in the market you should destroy the refreshment thence derived; much more ought we to act on this principle after the Communion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as it is, we do the contrary, and in this very way throw away all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while the profitable effect of what hath been said to us is not yet well fixed, the great force of the things that press upon us from without sweeps all entirely away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That this then may not be the case, when you retire from the Communion, you must account nothing more necessary than that you should put together the things that have been said to you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes, for it were the utmost folly for us, while we give up five and even six days to the business of this life, not to bestow on things spiritual so much as one day, or rather not so much as a small part of one day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See ye not our own children, that whatever lessons are given them, those they study throughout the whole day?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then let us do likewise, since otherwise we shall derive no profit from coming here, drawing water daily into a vessel with holes, and not bestowing on the retaining of what we have heard even so much earnestness as we plainly show with respect to gold and silver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For any one who has received a few pence both puts them into a bag and sets a seal thereon; but we, having given us oracles more precious than either gold or costly stones, and receiving the treasures of the Spirit, do not put them away in the storehouses of our soul, but thoughtlessly and at random suffer them to escape from our minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who then will pity us after all this, plotting against our own interests, and casting ourselves into so deep poverty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, that this may not be so, let us write it down an unalterable law for ourselves, for our wives, and for our children, to give up this one day of the week entire to hearing, and to the recollection of the things we have heard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus with greater aptness for learning shall we approach what is next to be said; and to us the labor will be less, and to you the profit greater, when, bearing in memory what hath been lately spoken, ye hearken accordingly to what comes afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no little doth this also contribute towards the understanding of what is said, when ye know accurately the connexion of the thoughts, which we are busy in weaving together for you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since it is not possible to set down all in one day, you must by continued remembrance make the things laid before you on many days into a kind of chain, and so wrap it about your soul: that the body of the Scriptures may appear entire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore let us not either to-day go on to the subjects set before us, without first recalling what was lately said to our memory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what are the things set before us to-day?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In a tone worthy of the wonder, with all his might he hath uttered his voice, saying, “Now all this was done.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when he saw the sea and the abyss of the love of God towards man, and that actually come to pass which never had been looked for, and nature’s laws broken, and reconciliations made, Him who is above all come down to him that is lower than all, and “the middle walls of partition broken,”and the impediments removed, and many more things than these done besides; in one word he hath put before us the miracle, saying, “Now all this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “think not,” saith he, “that these things are now determined upon; they were prefigured of old.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which same thing, Paul also everywhere labors to prove.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the angel proceeds to refer Joseph to Isaiah; in order that even if he should, when awakened, forget his own words, as newly spoken, he might by being reminded of those of the prophet, with which he had been nourished up continually, retain likewise the substance of what he had said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to the woman he mentioned none of these things, as being a damsel and unskilled in them, but to the husband, as being a righteous man and one who studied the prophets, from them he reasons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And before this he saith, “Mary, thy wife;” but now, when he hath brought the prophet before him, he then trusts him with the name of virginity; for Joseph would not have continued thus unshaken, when he heard from him of a virgin, unlesshe had first heard it also from Isaiah.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it was nothing novel that he was to hear out of the prophets,but what was familiar to him, and had been for a long time the subject of his meditations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause the angel, to make what he said easy to be received, brings in Isaiah.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And neither here doth he stop, but connects the discourse with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he doth not call the saying Isaiah’s, but that of the God of all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause he said not, “that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of Isaiah,” but “which was spoken of the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the mouth indeed was Isaiah’s, but the oracle was wafted from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith this oracle?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Behold, a virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call His name Emmanuel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How was it then, one may say, that His name was not called Emmanuel, but Jesus Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he said not, “thou shalt call,” but “they shall call,” that is, the multitude, and the issue of events.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For here he puts the event as a name: and this is customary in Scripture, to substitute the events that take place for names.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, to say, “they shall call” Him “Emmanuel,” means nothing else than that they shall see God amongst men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He hath indeed always been amongst men, but never so manifestly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if Jews are obstinate, we will ask the, when was the child called, “Make speed to the spoil, hasten the prey?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, they could not say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How is it then that the prophet said, “Call his name Maher-shalal-hash-baz?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, when he was born, there was a taking and dividing of spoils, therefore the event that took place in his time is put as his name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the city, too, it is said, shall be called “the city of righteousness, the faithful city Sion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet we nowhere find that the city was called “righteousness,” but it continued to be called Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, inasmuch as this came to pass in fact, when the city underwent a change for the better, on that account he saith it is so called.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when any event happens which marks out him who brings it to pass, or who is benefited by it, more clearly than his name, the Scripturespeaks of the truth of the event as being a name to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if, when their mouths are stopped on this point, they should seek another, namely, what is said touching Mary’s virginity, and should object to us other translators,saying, that they used not the term “virgin,” but “young woman;” in the first place we will say this, that the Seventy were justly entitled to confidence above all the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these made their translation after Christ’s coming, continuing to be Jews, and may justly be suspected as having spoken rather in enmity, and as darkening the prophecies on purpose; but the Seventy, as having entered upon this work an hundred years or more before the coming of Christ, stand clear from all such suspicion, and on account of the date, and of their number, and of their agreement,would have a better right to be trusted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But even if they bring in the testimony of those others, yet so the tokens of victory would be with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because the Scripture is wont to put the word “youth,” for “virginity;” and this with respect not to women only, but also to men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is said, “young men and maidens, old men with younger ones.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, speaking of the damsel who is attacked, it saith, “if the young woman cry out,”meaning the virgin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what goes before also establishes this interpretation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he doth not merely say, “Behold, the Virgin shall be with child,” but having first said, “Behold, the Lord Himself shall give you a sign,” then he subjoins, “Behold, the Virgin shall be with child.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereas, if she that was to give birth was not a virgin, but this happened in the way of marriage, what sort of sign would the event be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that which is a sign must of course be beyond the course of common events, it must be strange and extraordinary; else how could it be a sign?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then Joseph, being raised from sleep, did as the angel of the Lord had bidden him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou obedience, and a submissive mind?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou a soul truly wakened, and in all things incorruptible?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither when he suspected something painful or amiss could he endure to keep the Virgin with him; nor yet, after he was freed from this suspicion, could he bear to cast her out, but he rather keeps her with him, and ministers to the whole Dispensation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And took unto him Mary his wife.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how continually the evangelist uses this word, not willing that that mystery should be disclosed as yet, and annihilating that evil suspicion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when he had taken her, “he knew her not, till she had brought forth her first-born Son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He hath here used the word “till,” not that thou shouldest suspect that afterwards he did know her, but to inform thee that before the birth the Virgin was wholly untouched by man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why then, it may be said, hath he used the word, “till”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because it is usual in Scripture often to do this, and to use this expression without reference to limited times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so with respect to the ark likewise, it is said, “The raven returned not till the earth was dried up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet it did not return even after that time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when discoursing also of God, the Scripture saith, “From age until age Thou art,”not as fixing limits in this case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again when it is preaching the Gospel beforehand, and saying, “In his days shall righteousness flourish, and abundance of peace, till the moon be taken away,”it doth not set a limit to this fair part of creation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So then here likewise, it uses the word “till,” to make certain what was before the birth, but as to what follows, it leaves thee to make the inference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, what it was necessary for thee to learn of Him, this He Himself hath said; that the Virgin was untouched by man until the birth; but that which both was seen to be a consequence of the former statement, and was acknowledged, this in its turn he leaves for thee to perceive; namely, that not even after this, she having so become a mother, and having been counted worthy of a new sort of travail, and a child-bearing so strange, could that righteous man ever have endured to know her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he had known her, and had kept her in the place of a wife, how is it that our Lordcommits her, as unprotected, and having no one, to His disciple, and commands him to take her to his own home?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then, one may say, are James and the others called His brethren?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the same kind of way as Joseph himself was supposed to be husband of Mary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For many were the veils provided, that the birth, being such as it was, might be for a time screened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore even John so called them, saying, “For neither did His brethren believe in Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless they, who did not believe at first, became afterwards admirable, and illustrious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At least when Paul and they that were of his company were come up to Jerusalem about decrees,they went in straightway unto James.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he was so admired as even to be the first to be entrusted with the bishop’s office.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they say he gave himself up to such great austerity, that even his members became all of them as dead, and that from his continual praying, and his perpetual intercourse with the ground, his forehead became so callous as to be in no better state than a camel’s knees, simply by reason of his striking it so against the earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This man gives directions to Paul himself, when he was after this come up again to Jerusalem, saying,“Thou seest, brother, how many thousands there are of them that are come together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great was his understanding and his zeal, or rather so great the power of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they that mock Him when living, after His death are so filled with awe, as even to die for Him with exceeding readiness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such things most of all show the power of His resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this, you see, was the reason of the more glorious things being kept till afterwards, viz. that this proof might become indisputable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For seeing that even those who are admired amongst us in their life, when they are gone, are apt to be forgotten by us; how was it that they, who made light of this Man living, afterwards thought Him to be God, if He was but one of the many?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How was it that they consented even to be slain for His sake, unless they received His resurrection on clear proof?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things we tell you, that ye may not hear only, but imitate also his manly severity,his plainness of speech, his righteousness in all things; that no one may despair of himself, though hitherto he have been careless, that he may set his hopes on nothing else, after God’s mercy, but on his own virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if these were nothing the better for such a kindred, though they were of the same house and lineage with Christ, until they gave proof of virtue; what favor can we possibly receive, when we plead righteous kinsmen and brethren, unless we be exceeding dutiful,and have lived in virtue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As the prophet too said, intimating the selfsame thing, “A brother redeemeth not, shall a man redeem?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No, not although it were Moses, Samuel, Jeremiah.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear, for example, what God saith unto this last, “Pray not thou for this people, for I will not hear thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why marvellest thou if I hear not thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Though Moses himself and Samuel stood before me,” I would not receive their supplication for these men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, if it be Ezekiel who entreats, he will be told, “Though Noah stand forth, and Job, and Daniel, they shall deliver neither sons nor daughters.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though the patriarch Abraham be supplicating for them that are most incurably diseased, and change not, God will leave him and go His way,that he may not receive his cry in their behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though again it be Samuel who is doing this, He saith unto him, “Mourn not thou for Saul.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though for his own sister one entreat, when it is not fitting, he again shall have the same sort of answer as Moses, “If her father had but spit in her face.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then be looking open-mouthed towards others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is true, the prayers of the saints have the greatest power; on condition however of our repentance and amendment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since even Moses, who had rescued his own brother and six hundred thousand men from the wrath that was then coming upon them from God, had no power to deliver his sister;and yet the sin was not equal; for whereas she had done despite but to Moses, in that other case it was plain impiety, what they ventured on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this difficulty I leave for you; while that which is yet harder, I will try to explain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For why should we speak of his sister? since he who stood forth the advocate of so great a people had not power to prevail for himself, but after his countless toils, and sufferings, and his assiduity for forty years, was prohibited from setting foot on that land, touching which there had been so many declarations and promises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then was the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To grant this favor would not be profitable, but would, on the contrary, bring with it much harm, and would be sure to prove a stumbling-block to many of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if when they were merely delivered from Egypt, they forsook God, and sought after Moses, and imputed all to him; had they seen him also lead them into the land of promise, to what extent of impiety might they not have been cast away?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this reason also, let me add, neither was his tomb made known.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Samuel again was not able to save Saul from the wrath from above, yet he oftentimes preserved the Israelites.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Jeremiah prevailed not for the Jews, but some one else he did haply cover from evil by his prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Daniel saved the barbarians from slaughter,but he did not deliver the Jews from their captivity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in the Gospels too we shall see both these events come to pass, not in the case of different persons, but of the same; and the same man now prevailing for himself and now given up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who owed the ten thousand talents, though he had delivered himself from the danger by entreaty, yet again he prevailed not,and another on the contrary, who had before thrown himself away, afterwards had power to help himself in the greatest degree.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But who is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that devoured his Father’s substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that on the one hand, if we be careless, we shall not be able to obtain salvation, no not even by the help of others; if, on the other hand, we be watchful, we shall be able to do this by ourselves, and by ourselves rather than by others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes; for God is more willing to give His grace to us, than to others for us; that we by endeavoring ourselves to do away His wrath, may both enjoy confidence towards Him, and become better men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He had pity on the Canaanitish woman, thus He saved the harlot, thus the thief, when there was none to be mediator nor advocate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this I say, not that we may omit supplicating the saints, but to hinder our being careless, and entrusting our concerns to others only, while we fall back and slumber ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so when He said, “make to yourselves friends,”he did not stop at this only, but He added, “of the unrighteous mammon;” that so again the good work may be thine own; for it is nothing else but almsgiving which He hath here signified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, what is marvellous, neither doth He make a strict account with us, if we withdraw ourselves from injustice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what He saith is like this: “Hast thou gained ill? spend well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou gathered by unrighteousness? scatter abroad in righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet, what manner of virtue is this, to give out of such gains?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God, however, being full of love to man, condescends even to this and if we thus do, promises us many good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But we are so past all feeling, as not to give even of our unjust gain, but while plundering without end, if we contribute the smallest part, we think we have fulfilled all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard Paul saying, “He which soweth sparingly, shall reap also sparingly”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then dost thou spare?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, is the act an outlay? is it an expense?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, it is gain and good merchandise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where there is merchandise, there is also increase; where there is sowing, there is also reaping.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, if thou hadst to till a rich and deep soil, and capable of receiving much seed, wouldest both spend what thou hadst, and wouldest borrow of other men, accounting parsimony in such cases to be loss; but, when it is Heaven which thou art to cultivate, which is exposed to no variation of weather, and will surely repay thine outlay with abundant increase, thou art slow and backward, and considerest not that it is possible by sparing to lose, and by not sparing to gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Disperse therefore, that thou mayest not lose; keep not, that thou mayest keep; lay out, that thou mayest save; spend, that thou mayest gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thy treasures are to be hoarded, do not thou hoard them, for thou wilt surely cast them away; but entrust them to God, for thence no man makes spoil of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not thou traffic, for thou knowest not at all how to gain; but lend unto Him who gives an interest greater than the principal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lend, where is no envy, no accusation, nor evil design, nor fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lend unto Him who wants nothing, yet hath need for thy sake; who feeds all men, yet is an hungered, that thou mayest not suffer famine; who is poor, that thou mayest be rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lend there, where thy return cannot be death, but life instead of death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this usury is the harbinger of a kingdom, that, of hell; the one coming of covetousness, the other of self-denial; the one of cruelty, the other of humanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What excuse then will be ours, when having the power to receive more, and that with security, and in due season, and in great freedom, without either reproaches, or fears, or dangers, we let go these gains, and follow after that other sort, base and vile as they are, insecure and perishable, and greatly aggravating the furnace for us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing, nothing is baser than the usury of this world, nothing more cruel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, other persons’ calamities are such a man’s traffic; he makes himself gain of the distress of another, and demands wages for kindness, as though he were afraid to seem merciful, and under the cloak of kindness he digs the pitfall deeper, by the act of help galling a man’s poverty, and in the act of stretching out the hand thrusting him down, and when receiving him as in harbor, involving him in shipwreck, as on a rock, or shoal, or reef.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But what dost thou require?” saith one; “that I should give another for his use that money which I have got together, and which is to me useful, and demand no recompense?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Far from it: I say not this: yea, I earnestly desire that thou shouldest have a recompense; not however a mean nor small one, but far greater; for in return for gold, I would that thou shouldest receive Heaven for usury.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then shut thyself up in poverty, crawling about the earth, and demanding little for great?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, this is the part of one who knows not how to be rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when God in return for a little money is promising thee the good things that are in Heaven, and thou sayest, “Give me not Heaven, but instead of Heaven the gold that perisheth,” this is for one who wishes to continue in poverty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even as he surely who desires wealth and abundance will choose things abiding rather than things perishing; the inexhaustible, rather than such as waste away; much rather than little, the incorruptible rather than the corruptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so the other sort too will follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as he who seeks earth before Heaven, will surely lose earth also, so he that prefers Heaven to earth, shall enjoy both in great excellency.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that this may be the case with us, let us despise all things here, and choose the good things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus shall we obtain both the one and the other, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ; to whom be glory and might for ever and ever.
